text: We are too young now to make critical decisions
comments: I coded this as decision-making because it directly explains why boys are not yet fully autonomous. It is nuanced because the limitation is linked to age, not absolute lack of agency.

text: We think of going to school positively
comments: This is a general positive aspiration toward education, but not tied to one specific job.

text: This is the most important thing for us
comments: Schooling is described as centrally important for life progress, so this is a broad aspiration.

text: To be able to get a good employment one should be educated and smart
comments: This clearly links education to desirable employment, so I coded it as occupational aspiration.

text: Everyone completes secondary education
comments: This suggests broad attainment at least through secondary school, so no clear gap is visible here.

text: Most continue education at colleges (vocational school)
comments: This indicates continued education is common, so I coded this as no clear attainment gap.

text: Thatĺs the same with girls
comments: This directly indicates similar educational pathways for boys and girls.

text: Everybody wants to graduate from the University
comments: This is a direct aspiration for higher education.

text: But how things will work we are not sure
comments: This shows some uncertainty about attaining the aspiration, but not a clearly blocked path, so smaller gap.

text: We all try and study
comments: This is a broad statement of educational striving.

text: We hope we will enroll at the University
comments: Strong higher-education aspiration.

text: Yes, of course
comments: This is part of the response that parents show the same commitment to daughters and sons.

text: This is what life requires
comments: This implies equal education is seen as a social necessity.

text: All parents want to be proud of their children
comments: This supports the idea of equal investment in children, regardless of gender.

text: We know what to do in the field
comments: This shows boysĺ agricultural knowledge and familiarity with field tasks.

text: Digging, cleaning water lines, removing the tops of plants, opening water lines and many others
comments: Specific examples of agricultural knowledge and practice.

text: Bad health
comments: This is listed as one of the reasons youth end their education, so it is an attainment barrier.

text: Family problems and financial hardships
comments: These are explicitly ranked as the two most important reasons for ending education.

text: Someone wants to be a doctor
comments: Clear occupational aspiration.

text: They can work at the store
comments: This describes common occupational destinations after schooling.

text: computer club
comments: This is a specific work pathway and likely aspiration.

text: or photographic studio
comments: Another specific employment pathway.

text: But they should work in the field as well and help parents
comments: This suggests continued family agricultural obligation alongside other work, so nuanced labor role.

text: They should help the family in household
comments: This points to expected family-role obligations after study.

text: Find employment
comments: Direct broad employment aspiration.

text: Arrange some kind of a small business for the beginning
comments: Direct business aspiration.

text: Development of greenhouses
comments: New agricultural practice recognized in the community.

text: New kind of wheat
comments: New crop innovation recognized locally.

text: New tractors and combines
comments: Technology adoption recognized as important practice.

text: New varieties of wheat are an additional yield and money for the family
comments: Innovation is valued because it improves income and production.

text: the same way both for girls and for boys
comments: This indicates equal benefit of wheat innovation for both sexes.

text: New mechanisms will help in those businesses where men are mostly involved in
comments: Mechanized work is explicitly framed as menĺs domain.

text: Selling milk products is a more of a female business
comments: This clearly assigns milk-selling to women, confirming labor division.

text: Yes, if they have the opportunities and are interested in it, then yes they do
comments: This supports equal access to learning and trying innovations.

text: A lot of new kinds of seeds appeared in our district
comments: Direct explanation of agricultural change through seed innovation.

text: Wheat is planted more and more last 10 years
comments: Indicates changing cropping patterns over time.

text: Yes they are following, as there are separate works traditionally that are carried out by men (hardwork), by women (more household)
comments: Clear confirmation of traditional gender division of labor.

text: They wouldnĺt have any problems. They have done it before
comments: This opposes the idea that women would face major barriers growing and selling on their own.

text: Nothing bad would happen
comments: This opposes strong restrictions on men taking on opposite-sex-associated production/selling.

text: No, all family members are responsible for the same plot, they could not have different plots
comments: Opposes gendered separation of plots.

text: Man works on home garden very seldom
comments: Indicates some differentiated task pattern, but not full separation.

text: Women works on farm plot almost every day
comments: Confirms womenĺs regular labor burden on farm plots.

text: Everyone would work together; there is no separation as for mine or yours. Everything is common
comments: Strongly opposes separation of plots/labor by sex or person.

text: Each woman asks her husband or specialists. So do men
comments: Consultation happens for both sexes, so I coded this as nuanced rather than fully restrictive.

text: He would ask for more help but not less help on his plots
comments: Confirms womenĺs labor obligations remain despite their own activities.

text: Normal reputation
comments: Opposes stigma against women selling in the market.

text: We think he has a normal reputation as well
comments: Opposes stigma against men sharing childcare/housework.

text: He will be very glad
comments: Positive reaction to wifeĺs business success.

text: Gender equality is good
comments: Direct positive perception of gender equality.

text: Of course, it can be less pleasant for men since they are the heads in the family
comments: This introduces some discomfort with equality because men are seen as family heads, so nuanced.

text: They will decide everything jointly, in cooperation with each other
comments: This strongly supports joint household decision-making.

text: They will discuss and decide jointly, who has more time will sell their products
comments: Opposes fixed male/female selling roles by making it time-based and joint.

text: Sure, because the spouse would be thinking more about effectiveness of the innovation
comments: Positive orientation toward trying innovation and using effectiveness as criterion.

text: She will explain him everything and he will understand
comments: This suggests a woman can proceed with inherited money, but through persuasion, so nuanced asset control.

text: Easy. There is no need in getting permission or consent from wife
comments: Clear statement that men have stronger independent control over inherited resources.

text: Young married woman easily walk around
comments: Opposes mobility restrictions for married women in local market spaces.

text: Itĺs easy for women to sell products on the market
comments: Opposes the norm that market selling is difficult or inappropriate for women.

text: No, girls do not migrate
comments: Strong restriction on womenĺs work migration.

text: These types of girls are not respected in our village
comments: Direct stigma against girls who migrate for work and return.

text: We think that for girls of 19-21 years of ages, and boys 22 ľ 25 years of ages
comments: Different marriage ages indicate different gender expectations.

text: Boys have more chances to choose a girl whereas girls more often wait on being chosen by a good boy
comments: This reflects unequal agency in marriage choice, but not total absence of womenĺs role, so nuanced.

text: It should be a joint decision between young couples and parents
comments: Supports shared decision-making in marriage rather than parent-only control.

text: Women typically have their first child at the ages of 20 ľ 21
comments: This is a normative statement of timing rather than clear equality/inequality; I coded it as a weaker normative expectation.

text: Yes. At present time we have no cases of early marriage
comments: Positive framing of later marriage and childbirth.

text: Usually girls are married after completing colleges
comments: Suggests support for girls finishing education before marriage.

text: 21-22 year old girls could have their first children
comments: States preferred age for first child.

text: Many good and attractive buildings, schools, nice roads, streets
comments: Broad aspiration for village development and infrastructure.

text: More new employment seats, education facilities, sports compounds
comments: Broad aspiration for jobs, education, and youth facilities.
